what_it_does: Implements the Workshop AI engine (Vi) for ConceptV: a pure functional diff/transform layer, LLM prompt builders for block/page/widget/wizard edits and suggestions, candidate gallery and diff-card UI primitives, prompt caching, diff inversion, streaming generation, workspace variable substitution, a global docs store, and a per-field regeneration toolbar.
observations: Defines a typed diff schema (block.*, page.*, doc.*, widget.*, wizard.step.*, batch) and a pure applyDiff implementation.

Provides many Vi prompt builders (viInsertBlock, viAlternateBlock, viInsertPage, viDocTransform, viThemeGenerate, viLayoutGenerate, viWidgetAlternate, viWidgetKPIsRegen, viWizardStepInsert, viWizardStepAlternate, viFieldAlternate) that call window.claude.complete.
Includes UI components WSCandidateGallery, WSDiffCard, WSTransformMenu, and several Mini* preview components.
Maintains a global imperative BRIDGE singleton so any surface can read/write the active doc.
Implements a 60-second prompt cache (PROMPT_CACHE) keyed by a simple string hash.
Implements invertDiff to produce undo diffs for every operation including batches.
Supports parallel streaming candidate generation for certain target kinds via generateCandidatesStream.
Provides workspace variable substitution ({{key}}), a variable store, reference counting, and an LLM-driven audit to suggest new variables.
Maintains DOCS_STORE, a global subscribable store of all docs for live cross-doc embeds.
Includes a floating WSFieldToolbar for per-field alternate generation and promotion to workspace variables.
Uses window globals (WS_BLOCKS, claude, dsaToast) rather than module imports.
standouts: Diff-based generative editing with inverse diffs for independent undo/redo.
Parallel streaming candidate generation with per-slot angle seeds.
Workspace-wide variable system with substitution, reference counting, and Vi audit.
Global bridge and docs store enabling cross-surface/cross-doc live updates.
Per-field floating toolbar that turns any input into an AI regen target.
conventions: Functional React components using aliased hooks (useState_ai, useEffect_ai, etc.).
Global window.WS_* registries and singleton stores instead of React context.
Pure functions for document transforms; diffs are immutable plain objects.
LLM prompts request JSON-only responses and parse them with a loose extractor.
Prompt caching keyed by a simple rolling hash with TTL and size cap.
IDs generated with Date.now + Math.random rather than a UUID library.
Inline UI primitives mixed with engine logic in a single file.
concerns: Heavy reliance on window globals (WS_BLOCKS, claude, dsaToast) with no visible imports or type contracts.
applyDiff uses Object.assign and shallow merges in places, and mutates the cloned doc via splice/push, which could surprise callers expecting deep immutability.
ID generation uses Date.now + Math.random and is neither collision-safe nor deterministic.
parseJsonLoose regex may extract the wrong JSON object from a malformed LLM reply.
PROMPT_CACHE hash is a simple 32-bit rolling hash and may collide for different prompts.
cachedComplete has an ambiguous signature: it sometimes passes opts to claude.complete but the prompt argument is not always wrapped correctly.
LocalStorage dismissal key is global and not scoped per workspace or user.
WSFieldToolbar uses browser prompt() for variable key input and depends on window.dsaToast.
The file is large and mixes engine, UI, stores, and global side effects, increasing coupling.
generateOneCandidate passes seededTarget unchanged and appends an angle string to brief, but does not guarantee distinct outputs.
notes: A single file that tries to be the entire AI backend and its UI shell for Workshop, using a diff protocol to keep LLM-generated changes previewable and reversible.
questions: How is window.claude.complete implemented and authenticated?
Where are window.WS_BLOCKS defined and how are their renderers registered?
How is React provided to this module (global vs import)?
How do theme keys map to actual CSS/design tokens?
What is the persistence lifecycle of DOCS_STORE and VARS_STORE?
How does the system handle LLM outputs that omit required fields or use unknown block kinds?
How are candidate previews rendered for block kinds not explicitly handled in MiniBlockPreview?
How does generateCandidatesStream coordinate busy/loading state with the gallery UI?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header states it centralizes everything Vi can do inside Workshop, and the file indeed contains the diff engine, generation dispatch, UI primitives, bridge, stores, and variable system.
apparent_themes: Diffs as the universal currency between LLM and document.
LLM as a candidate generator, not a direct mutator.
Global imperative bridges for cross-surface coordination.
Streaming and caching for perceived performance.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make every AI-driven edit in ConceptV Workshop go through one shared, previewable, reversible diff pipeline, while also giving individual fields and whole documents quick access to Vi via global registries and UI primitives.
novelty: A self-contained AI engine module that combines typed diff application/inversion, parallel streaming candidate generation, workspace variable auditing, and a per-field AI toolbar in a single file.
contribution: It contributes a concrete diff schema and apply/invert pair, a candidate gallery and diff-card UI for previewing and accepting AI edits, a workspace variable substitution and audit mechanism, and a global bridge/store pattern that lets cross-doc embeds stay live.
summary_for_embedding: AIEngine.jsx is the Workshop AI engine for ConceptV, providing a typed diff system, LLM-based generation for blocks/pages/widgets/wizards, candidate and diff UI components, prompt caching, streaming, inverse diffs, workspace variables, and global doc/bridge stores.
intention_for_embedding: It was built to centralize all Vi-driven generative editing in Workshop behind a uniform, previewable, and reversible diff protocol.

symbols.does: Deep-clones a value using JSON serialization.
symbols.description: Returns a clone of the input object via JSON.parse(JSON.stringify), passing null through unchanged. It is used throughout the file to create mutable copies of the document before applying diffs.

symbols.does: Applies a typed diff or batch of diffs to a document.
symbols.description: Clones the document and then mutates the clone according to the diff kind, supporting block, page, doc, widget, wizard step, and batch operations. It returns the new document or the original if the diff is invalid.

symbols.does: Normalizes a section object with defaults and an id.
symbols.description: Takes a section, looks up its kind in window.WS_BLOCKS, and returns an object with a generated id, kind, merged default data, and locked flag. It is used to make LLM-generated sections conform to the block registry.

symbols.does: Produces a human-readable summary of a diff.
symbols.description: Handles batch diffs recursively and returns short labels such as 'Insert a headline block' or 'Update page 2: title, theme'. It is used in the candidate gallery and diff cards to explain changes.

symbols.does: Returns the display label for a block kind.
symbols.description: Looks up the block definition in window.WS_BLOCKS and returns its label, falling back to the raw kind string. It supports describeDiff and candidate summaries.

symbols.does: Parses JSON from an LLM reply, with a fallback extractor.
symbols.description: Attempts strict JSON.parse first; on failure it extracts the first {...} substring and tries again. It is the standard way the file recovers from prose-wrapped model output.

symbols.does: Lists registered block kinds except divider.
symbols.description: Returns the keys of window.WS_BLOCKS filtered to exclude the 'divider' kind. It is used when building prompts so Vi only sees insertable block types.

symbols.does: Builds a compact prompt description of all block data shapes.
symbols.description: Iterates available block kinds and emits a line per kind showing its label and default data keys. The result is included in generation prompts to guide the model.

symbols.does: Summarizes pages near a target page for prompts.
symbols.description: Formats the target page and its immediate neighbors as a concise string of page titles and section kinds/data. It gives Vi local context without sending the whole document.

symbols.does: Formats a single page for pageContextSummary.
symbols.description: Returns a string containing the page index, title or kind, and a truncated representation of each section. It is the inner helper used by pageContextSummary.

symbols.does: Dispatches generation requests to the appropriate Vi function.
symbols.description: Inspects target.kind and routes to one of the vi* generators, returning a list of candidate objects. It is the main entry point for producing AI edit candidates.

symbols.does: Constructs a candidate object.
symbols.description: Returns a candidate with a random id, label, optional summary, and diff. It is defined in generateCandidates but the dispatch functions build candidates directly.

symbols.does: Generates a short random id string.
symbols.description: Returns an 8-character base-36 random string. It is used for candidate ids and some generated section/page ids.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi to propose blocks to insert at a page position.
symbols.description: Builds a context-rich prompt from the document type, nearby pages, available block shapes, and user brief, then parses candidate blocks and returns block.insert diffs.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi for alternate versions of an existing block.
symbols.description: Sends the current block data and sibling context to the model, asking for different angles while keeping the same shape, and returns block.replace diffs.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi to propose new pages at a given slot.
symbols.description: Provides pages before and after the insertion point plus available block kinds, then parses full page candidates into page.insert diffs.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi for whole-document transforms.
symbols.description: Flattens the top blocks of the document, sends the user instruction, and parses a list of block updates into a batch diff.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi for alternative visual themes.
symbols.description: Asks the model to pick from known theme keys or propose custom names, then returns doc.update diffs setting the theme field.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi for full page layouts.
symbols.description: Sends page context and available block shapes, asks for ordered block lists, and returns a batch diff that clears and repopulates the page sections.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi for full-widget data alternatives.
symbols.description: Sends current widget kind, system, and data, asks for different angles, and returns widget.data diffs.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi for alternative KPI sets.
symbols.description: Sends existing KPIs and a desired count, asks for different angles, and returns widget.data diffs containing new kpis arrays.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi to propose new wizard steps.
symbols.description: Sends existing step summaries and insertion position, asks for a single step, and returns wizard.step.insert diffs.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi for alternate versions of a wizard step.
symbols.description: Sends the current step and sibling titles, asks for different versions, and returns wizard.step.update diffs.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi for alternative values of a single field.
symbols.description: Sends block kind, field name, current value, and an angle hint, asks for alternatives of the same JSON type, and returns candidate objects with value but no diff.

symbols.does: Classifies a chat message as edit or question.
symbols.description: Sends the document type, title, and user message to the model and returns a JSON kind/reason object. It is used by chat-rail edit mode.

symbols.does: Generates a batch edit diff from a natural-language request.
symbols.description: Builds a prompt containing document contents and available op kinds, parses the model's JSON ops, and wraps them in a batch diff with a summary.

symbols.does: Removes ids and timestamps before templating.
symbols.description: Clones the document and deletes id and createdAt at the document, page, section, and step levels. It keeps template extraction stable across runs.

symbols.does: Uses Vi to turn a document into a parameterized template.
symbols.description: Strips volatile fields, sends a capped JSON corpus, asks the model to identify variables and replace occurrences with {{key}} placeholders, and returns variables plus the templated doc.

symbols.does: Fills a template with values and restamps ids.
symbols.description: Stringifies the templated doc, replaces placeholders with supplied values, parses it back, and assigns new ids and createdAt to the document, pages, sections, and steps.

symbols.does: Escapes special regex characters in a string.
symbols.description: Returns the input string with regex metacharacters prefixed by backslash. It is used when building placeholder replacement regexes.

symbols.does: Produces heuristic suggestions based on document state.
symbols.description: Inspects the current page and overall document to suggest actions like filling an empty page, adding a CTA, adding stats, or whole-doc transforms. It is used by viSuggest and the UI.

symbols.does: React hook that subscribes to the active bridge document.
symbols.description: Returns the current BRIDGE.doc and forces a re-render whenever the bridge notifies. It lets components react to the live active document.

symbols.does: Modal gallery for picking an AI-generated candidate.
symbols.description: Renders candidate cards with previews, keyboard navigation, placeholder skeletons while busy, and a refine input to steer regeneration. It is the primary UI for accepting Vi proposals.

symbols.does: Keyboard navigation handler for the candidate gallery.
symbols.description: Handles Escape to close, arrow keys to move focus, Enter or number keys to pick a candidate, and ignores input fields. It is registered inside WSCandidateGallery.

symbols.does: Renders a preview of a candidate's diff.
symbols.description: Applies the candidate diff to the current doc and chooses an appropriate mini or rendered preview based on diff kind, including block, page, batch, widget, and wizard step previews.

symbols.does: Small preview component for common block kinds.
symbols.description: Renders a compact representation of headline, hero, body, quote, stats, callout, bullets, metricRow, and statPills blocks, falling back to a JSON snippet for unknown kinds.

symbols.does: Small preview stub for a page.
symbols.description: Shows the page title and a row of block-kind labels for the first few sections. It is used when previewing page.insert candidates.

symbols.does: Small preview for widget data.
symbols.description: Displays the widget eyebrow, title, and up to three KPIs. It is used for widget.data candidates.

symbols.does: Small preview for a wizard step.
symbols.description: Shows the step kind, title, body, and a few fields or options. It is used for wizard step insert/update candidates.

symbols.does: Card showing a proposed edit with apply/discard actions.
symbols.description: Renders the edit summary, a list of WSDiffLine previews, and buttons to apply or discard. It supports expanding batches with more than three diffs.

symbols.does: Renders a single diff with before/after or insertion preview.
symbols.description: For block updates and replacements it shows side-by-side before and after rendered previews; for inserts it shows the new block or page; otherwise it falls back to a text description.

symbols.does: Menu of whole-document transform presets.
symbols.description: Renders DOC_TRANSFORMS items such as shorten, urgent, friendly, and audit, calling onPickTransform when the user selects one.

symbols.does: Caches LLM completions for 60 seconds.
symbols.description: Hashes the prompt, returns a cached value if fresh, otherwise calls window.claude.complete and stores the result. It is used by theme generation, field alternates, and viSuggest.

symbols.does: Computes the inverse of a diff for undo.
symbols.description: Walks batches in order, builds inverses for each sub-diff, and handles every supported diff kind by producing the opposite operation. It enables undo/redo independent of React state.

symbols.does: Streams candidates as they arrive from the model.
symbols.description: For certain target kinds it fires parallel single-candidate calls and invokes onCandidate for each result; otherwise it falls back to the batched generateCandidates. It improves perceived speed.

symbols.does: Generates a single candidate with an angle seed.
symbols.description: Appends a seed angle and slot index to the brief and calls generateCandidates with count 1. It is the unit of work used by the streaming path.

symbols.does: Reads dismissed suggestion ids from localStorage.
symbols.description: Parses the cvstudio:dismissedSuggestions localStorage entry into a Set, returning an empty set on failure. It powers suggestion dismissal.

symbols.does: Marks a suggestion id as dismissed in localStorage.
symbols.description: Adds the id to the dismissed set and persists it back to localStorage.

symbols.does: Removes a suggestion id from the dismissed set.
symbols.description: Deletes the id from localStorage-backed dismissed suggestions.

symbols.does: Combines heuristic and Vi-generated suggestions.
symbols.description: Filters heuristic suggestions by dismissal state, asks the model for more suggestions, and interleaves Vi suggestions with heuristic ones up to a cap.

symbols.does: Renders a real block component at reduced scale.
symbols.description: Looks up the block renderer in window.WS_BLOCKS, wraps it in a scaled container with pointer events disabled, and passes a no-op setter. It gives faithful candidate previews.

symbols.does: No-op function used as a placeholder setter.
symbols.description: An empty arrow function passed to block renderers so contentEditable callbacks do not mutate the preview document.

symbols.does: Renders a real page at reduced scale.
symbols.description: Scales the page title and up to six section renderers into a compact preview container. It is used for page-level candidate previews.

symbols.does: React hook subscribing to the global docs store.
symbols.description: Returns the current array of docs and re-renders whenever DOCS_STORE changes. It supports live cross-doc embeds.

symbols.does: Replaces {{key}} placeholders in a string with variable values.
symbols.description: Accepts an optional vars array or falls back to VARS_STORE.values, then replaces every placeholder with the corresponding value. It is the runtime side of workspace variables.

symbols.does: React hook subscribing to workspace variables.
symbols.description: Returns the current variables array and re-renders whenever VARS_STORE changes.

symbols.does: Counts how many times each variable is referenced across docs.
symbols.description: Serializes all docs and counts total placeholder occurrences per variable key, plus the number of distinct docs containing each key. It supports variable management UI.

symbols.does: Asks Vi to identify values worth promoting to workspace variables.
symbols.description: Collects a sample of string values from all docs, sends them with existing variable keys, and parses proposed variables with keys, labels, values, kinds, and rationales.

symbols.does: Recursively collects string values from a document for the variable audit.
symbols.description: Traverses objects and arrays, skipping id and createdAt, and records each string value with its path. It is the collector used by auditWorkspaceVariables.

symbols.does: Floating toolbar for per-field AI actions.
symbols.description: Appears above the focused field and offers alternate, shorter, formal, specific, and promote-to-variable actions. It is the UI entry point for field-level regen.

symbols.does: Measures and follows the active field's bounding rect.
symbols.description: Effect inside WSFieldToolbar that updates the toolbar position on scroll, resize, and layout changes. It keeps the toolbar anchored to the field.

symbols.does: Handles toolbar regen button clicks.
symbols.description: Prevents default, invokes the registered regenHandler with the chosen angle and active field info, then deactivates the toolbar.

symbols.does: Promotes the current field value to a workspace variable.
symbols.description: Prompts for a variable key, creates or updates the variable in VARS_STORE, calls onApply with the placeholder, and shows a toast. It enables global reuse of field values.

symbols.does: Attaches field-toolbar behavior to a plain input or textarea.
symbols.description: Adds focus and blur listeners that activate/deactivate the toolbar with the element's value, apply callback, and metadata. It returns an unbind function.

symbols.does: Focus handler for bindFieldRegen.
symbols.description: Captures the element's bounding rect and activates the field toolbar with the current value and metadata.

symbols.does: Blur handler for bindFieldRegen.
symbols.description: Delays deactivation so clicks on the toolbar itself do not immediately dismiss it.
